items.note: Title identifies a diagnostic-accuracy study and names accuracy measures (sensitivity/specificity/AUC implied via 'diagnostic accuracy ... classifiers').
Structured abstract: design, participants, index tests, reference standard, N, accuracy estimates with 95% CIs, conclusions.
Intro gives clinical background of FNA and intended discriminative use.
Intro states two explicit aims (accuracy estimation + classifier comparison).
Analysis is retrospective on a fixed public benchmark; 'held-out test set' design stated, but the prospective/retrospective label is not given verbatim (benchmark has no data-collection timing).
Dataset/source and feature set described; classic inclusion/exclusion criteria and clinical setting do not apply to a curated benchmark and are not stated as such.
Stratified random train/test split described; original consecutive/random/convenience series of the source cohort not characterized (benchmark provenance).
Three index tests fully specified (model family, 400 trees, RBF kernel, standardization fit on train only, probability output) -- replicable with code + seed.
Reference standard = histopathologic diagnosis (malignant/benign), stated in Methods + Abstract.
Histopathology used as the diagnostic gold standard; rationale is implicit (no alternative considered/justified).
0.5 probability threshold pre-specified; Youden alternative reported and labeled as alternative operating threshold.
Reference standard is a binary histopathologic diagnosis; no cut-off applies.

No human readers; index tests are deterministic given the held-out features. Train/test separation prevents leakage; blinding in the STARD sense does not apply to an automated classifier on a labeled benchmark.
Methods specify AUC with DeLong CIs, Wilson CIs for proportions, Brier calibration, and pairwise DeLong comparison of AUCs.
Test n=171 from a 70/30 split is stated, but no formal power/precision justification is given.
Public benchmark dataset; recruitment dates/setting do not apply and are unavailable.
Class-level feature characteristics (Table-1-style means/SDs/SMDs) reported; patient demographics (age/sex) are absent from the benchmark.
N=569 -> train 398 / test 171; malignant/benign counts given; STARD flow diagram provided (Figure 1).
Index test and reference standard are computed/recorded simultaneously on the same archived sample; no time interval applies.
Distribution by class is described via feature SMDs; disease severity spectrum / alternative diagnoses not characterized (benchmark has only binary label).
2x2 cross-tabulation reported: TP/FP/FN/TN for the logistic-regression index test (Figure 1 + Results) and confusion matrices for all three (Figure 3).
Retrospective computational analysis; no procedures performed, no adverse events possible.
AUC with DeLong 95% CIs and Sens/Spec/PPV/NPV/Acc with Wilson CIs reported per index test.
No indeterminate results: every probability maps to positive/negative at the threshold; dataset has no missing labels/features.
No subgroup analyses performed; none claimed. (Single comparison across the three index tests.)
Discussion explicitly states single-source benchmark, high prevalence, no external validation, conventional threshold, and the unverified-references/methods-demo caveat.
Discussion states intended interpretive role and explicitly bounds clinical applicability (not transportable; methods demo).
No registration number; benchmark re-analysis is not registered (note: registration is increasingly expected).
Full analysis code + reproducibility lock accompany the demo (Data Availability), but no separately accessible study protocol is cited.
Funding statement present on title page ('None').
